market_regime.description: Normal volatility - standard setups work well

reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
COT extreme_long — strong contrarian signal available

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (9.38) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 4.0% move to $376.88 (weekly expected move: 8.6%).

thesis: Momentum is the dominant signal here — scoring 100 with trend persistence at 95 indicates a strong, sustained move rather than a spike. In normal VIX (all multipliers at 1.0x baseline), momentum carries its full 0.20 weight without regime headwinds, and all three ensemble models should agree on a momentum+trend combination this clean. Kill condition: if PANW gaps down >2% at Monday open (skip per gap-stop rule) or if the software/cloud sector sells off on any rotation out of narrow leadership names — with breadth showing narrow_leadership, PANW's large-cap status is actually favorable, but a breadth regime shift mid-week would undermine the thesis.

reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
COT extreme_long — strong contrarian signal available

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (4.97) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 0.6% move to $259.97 (weekly expected move: 5.0%).

thesis: JNJ is a trend-persistence play (85) in a defensive sector that benefits from the current narrow-leadership breadth regime — large-cap quality names attract flows when market breadth is thin. The strike efficiency score of 75 indicates favorable liquidity and bid-ask spreads for weekly options execution, which matters given our 15% execution tier weight. Kill condition: JNJ is sensitive to drug pricing headlines or FDA actions — any negative regulatory news during the holding window invalidates the steady-trend thesis. Also watch for charm decay: JNJ's lower volatility means delta will erode faster in percentage terms by Wednesday.

market_regime.description: Normal volatility - standard setups work well

reasons: VIX regime stable — good for weekly holds
COT extreme_long — strong contrarian signal available

entry.instruction: Enter Monday morning. Wait 15-20 min after open for spread to tighten. Limit order at mid (1.01) or better. Skip if gap > 2%.

exit.instruction: Time-decay-aware exits: take 40% day-1 / 35% day-2 / 25% day-3 / 15% day-4 profit. Hard stop at 50% loss. Exit if delta < 0.10. Breakeven requires 1.6% move to $19.99 (weekly expected move: 7.5%).

thesis: T is a momentum-driven bearish play with trend persistence (95) confirming the downtrend is established, not a one-day dip. This pick serves double duty: it's the highest-conviction put candidate AND it provides critical direction balance — running three calls into a week with COT at extreme_long (100th percentile, strong contrarian bearish signal) and tight credit spreads (complacent market) would leave the book fully exposed to a risk-off move. Kill condition: if T bounces above its 20-day SMA at Monday open, the bearish trend thesis is weakening; also invalidated if telecom sector catches a bid on any defensive rotation (which would contradict the put direction).
